title: ‘Hubie Halloween’ and 25 Horror Movies Classics
format: Sean and Chris check the state of 2020 horror, review Adam Sandler's 'Hubie Halloween,' then trade a 20-deep countdown of under-seen scary movies, each pitched by its premise.

segments.heading: Horror in the terrifying year of 2020

films.title: Host
films.note: Their pick for horror movie of the year, 'if only by circumstance' — a séance-over-Zoom feature born entirely of lockdown.

films.title: Possessor
films.note: Brandon Cronenberg's extreme body horror; Sean interviewed him the week before.

films.title: The Wolf of Snow Hollow
films.note: Jim Cummings's small-town monster-movie riff, a follow-up in spirit to 'Thunder Road.'

films.title: Bad Hair
films.note: Justin Simien's '70s/'80s-influenced horror satire, hitting Hulu the week of taping.

films.title: Synchronic
films.note: Benson and Moorhead's time-travel sci-fi with Anthony Mackie and Jamie Dornan; a sister film to 'The Endless.'

films.title: The Dark and the Wicked
films.note: Brian Bertino ('The Strangers') returns with what Sean calls one of the most depraved movies he's seen; out that November.

segments.heading: 'Hubie Halloween'
segments.note: the new Adam Sandler Netflix vehicle

films.title: Hubie Halloween
films.note: Stephen Brill directs Sandler as Salem's bullied Halloween safety coordinator. Sean sees a return to form; Chris was 'on mushrooms' at the cast of walk-ons and Ray Liotta's presence.

segments.heading: 20 underrated scary-movie classics
segments.note: counting down 10 to 1, one pick per host, each pitched by its premise

picks.title: The Houses October Built
picks.note: No. 10 — found-footage RV trip across Texas hunting an underground haunted house. Chris compared the dread to first seeing 'Texas Chain Saw.'

picks.title: La Llorona
picks.note: No. 10 — Jayro Bustamante's Guatemalan film folds the folk legend into a general's genocide trial, protest as haunting. On Shudder. Not the Conjuring-universe 'Curse of La Llorona.'

picks.title: As Above, So Below
picks.note: No. 9 — a treasure hunt gone wrong in the Paris catacombs; a solid C-plus scare on Netflix.

picks.title: Salem's Lot
picks.note: No. 9 — Tobe Hooper's three-hour Stephen King miniseries; Sean rates the window reveal the scariest moment ever on broadcast TV.

picks.title: Pontypool
picks.note: No. 8 — Canadian one-location horror: a radio DJ works through a language-borne virus. 'It's such a great movie.'

picks.title: Witchfinder General
picks.note: No. 8 — Michael Reeves at 25; Vincent Price hangs witches during the English Civil War. Brutal, hard to find, 85 minutes.

picks.title: The Bay
picks.note: No. 7 — Barry Levinson's eco found-footage horror on a July 4th weekend in Chesapeake Bay; a 'JV Cloverfield' with Heather Donahue.

picks.title: Halloween III: Season of the Witch
picks.note: No. 7 — the Michael-Myers-free anthology entry: Silver Shamrock masks and Tom Atkins. The franchise's original vision.

picks.title: House of the Devil
picks.note: No. 6 — Ti West's slow-burn '80s throwback: a broke babysitter takes a too-good gig at Tom Noonan's house. Early Greta Gerwig.

picks.title: Def by Temptation
picks.note: No. 6 — James Bond III's all-Black indie: a succubus (Cynthia Bond) preys on a preacher's son. Shot by Ernest Dickerson, with Samuel L. Jackson and Kadeem Hardison.

picks.title: Witches in the Woods
picks.note: No. 5 — teens stranded in a Jeep on a haunted mountainside; Chris singles out an inventive 360-degree interior shot. On Showtime.

picks.title: Rabid
picks.note: No. 5 — early Cronenberg outbreak movie with Marilyn Chambers; on Kanopy and the Criterion Channel. COVID-brain viewing.

picks.title: Stake Land
picks.note: Moved up to pair with 'Rabid' — Jim Mickle's road movie across a vampire-ravaged America. 'The Walking Dead, but good'; a fucked-up 'Goonies.'

picks.title: Tales from the Darkside: The Movie
picks.note: No. 4 — Romero-lineage horror anthology; a stacked cast (Buscemi, Julianne Moore, Christian Slater) and a nasty transformation. Free on Pluto TV.

picks.title: Southbound
picks.note: No. 3 — interlocking anthology on a cursed Texas highway; segments by Radio Silence, Roxanne Benjamin and David Bruckner.

picks.title: The Ghost Ship
picks.note: No. 3 — Val Lewton production directed by Mark Robson; claustrophobic shipboard dread. On Vudu.

picks.title: Pod
picks.note: No. 2 — micro-budget siblings-vs-basement-alien creeper from Mickey Keating, produced by and featuring Larry Fessenden. On Shudder.

picks.title: REC
picks.note: No. 2 — the Spanish found-footage landmark Alex Ross Perry named the genre's best. Seek the subtitled cut.

picks.title: Hell House LLC
picks.note: No. 1 — found-footage crew stages a haunted house in an abandoned upstate hotel; a whole trilogy Chris never knew existed. On Shudder.

picks.title: Let's Scare Jessica to Death
picks.note: No. 1 (a 'cheat' pick) — gateway to the Criterion Channel's '70s social-paranoia horror wave.

films: What We Do in the Shadows
Once Upon a Time in Hollywood
